relatedWork: Reflexive Journals — Mural integration (follow-up to the 2026-06-12 layout fix)
reportedDefects: Synced journal cards lost the board's Snowberry project tag (Test Project 1).
User-created ResearchOps tags did not render with Mural's Mint background and border colour.
project-context.js logged 401 authentication_required from /api/projects/:id, so project-route hydration failed on the preview.

rootCause.snowberryTagLoss: The server only carries the board project tag onto a card when the client-sent projectName text-matches the tag. When project-route hydration fails, main[data-project-name] is empty and the client sends the project id, so the project-name tag filter drops the Snowberry tag. The Mural widget PATCH also replaces the whole tag set, so applying a list missing the project tag stripped Snowberry from a patched template sticky.
rootCause.tagsNotMint: The safe-tags wrapper only applied the Mint style at tag creation. User tags already present on the board from earlier sync attempts were reused as-is with their original default style.

implementationSummary: Resolve the project name server-side from the D1 projects and rops_projects_cache records, checking all Test Project 1 id aliases (canonical, legacy, local); keep the client-sent name as a fallback. Names longer than Mural's 25-character tag limit match their truncated board tag.

Merge the tags already on a widget into PATCH tag application (resolved by id or text, including widget tag objects whose ids are absent from the board tag list) so a PATCH cannot strip the Raspberry category or Snowberry project tag from an existing sticky.

Restyle user-authored ResearchOps tags that already exist with non-Mint styling to the Mint contract via PATCH /murals/{muralId}/tags/{tagId}; never restyle board-curated category or project tags; update the in-request tag cache after a successful restyle so the same tag is not re-patched on every write.
Align the compact journal Mural sync payload with the journal tabs payload so both prefer the hydrated main[data-project-name] value before falling back to the page heading or project id.
Add this trace artefact dated 2026-06-14 to satisfy the feature-branch trace-coverage gate, which keys required traces to the CI run date.

Place pending cards on the Reflexive Journal template's fixed grid by category row index (columns at x 120/456/792/1128, cards 288x168, first row y 264, 192px row pitch) instead of using the latest existing widget y as the anchor. This prevents stale, oversized, merged or overlapping widgets from causing stacked cards. The first/template card PATCH receives the same fixed placement as newly created cards, so row 0 is also pinned to x/y/width/height. Matching still uses the tolerant derived layout so existing/hand-placed cards are recognised; only pending card placement is pinned to the grid. The implementation does not send literal Auto for font sizing because that value was rejected by Mural.

Make isTemplatePlaceholder and latestCanonicalWidget use marker-stripped content (bodyTextWithoutEntryMarkers) so a blank template carrying a stale journal-entry marker is recognised as a placeholder and not treated as the latest synced card; the first pending entry patches the template instead of being created below it leaving the template blank. Covered with a runtime test that a blank template with a stale title+tag marker is patched (updated-template-widget). Perceptions horizontal misalignment on the live board is pre-existing-card pollution, not a placement-code defect; fresh syncs produce aligned columns.

Require canonicalExistingWidget candidates to hold real entry text — visible text with any journal-entry:<id> marker stripped must be non-empty — before matching by marker or by body. The status annotation injects the marker into the widget title and normalizeWidget folds the title into .text, so a naive non-empty check is still fooled; stripping the marker is what identifies the card as blank. This corrects the status over-count (6 of 36 reported when only 3 cards existed, persisting after the first attempt) and the hydrate idempotency check, and was covered with a blank-template-with-stale-marker (title and tags) status regression test.

Preserve the known-good tag/content request shape from 87459b1fbba36836d504ecec979811f28a6859a0 while keeping the fixed card grid from bdae4111490da4a50c6700033388d5d144828fff. Template-card updates now send placement-only PATCHes first (fixed x/y/width/height, with x/y fallback for strict payloads) and then send the existing content/tag PATCH body without placement fields, so tag handling is not affected by placement fields.

validationRun.summary: Includes fixed-grid coverage for template-card placement and asserts the content/tag PATCH keeps the 87459b1f shape without placement fields.
Includes Mint restyle/tag-preservation coverage and asserts template-card placement is separated from tag/content writes.
Passed once a 2026-06-14 trace directory existed.

residualRisks: No live Mural board mutation was performed from this session; coverage is via mocked Mural API responses.

Mural does not publicly document its built-in tag preset colour values. The Mint constants in mural-journal-sync-safe-tags.js (#DDF7E8FF background, #98DDB8FF border, #0B0C0CFF text) are the single place to adjust if the workspace's built-in Mint preset differs.

The trace-coverage gate keys the required trace directory to the CI run date (UTC); a CI re-run on a later calendar day will require a trace directory for that day. This is existing repository behaviour, not introduced by this change.
